Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
Administratian 
322·5660 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
Enfarcement 
322-6441 
December 8, 1982 
Henry S. Barbosa 
Attorney at Law 
Oviatt Building 
617 South Olive 
Los Angeles, CA 
Street, Suite 100 
90014 
Re: 
Our No. A-82-198 
Dear Mr. Barbosa: 
Statements af Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of 
Montebello City Councilmembers Nighswonger, Molinari and 
Hensle. 
The facts, as I understand them, are that the City 
is considering adopting a rent mediation ordinance. 
This 
will involve the creation of a mediation board to hear 
disputes between landlords and tenants over proposed rent 
increases. 
Specifically, the ordinance will: 
1) Allow 
either a landlord or a tenant to request mediation of a 
rent dispute. 
The board's decision will be nonbinding on 
either party, and even after a negative decision a landlord 
may proceed with the rent increase. 
2) 
Refusal of a 
landlord. to attend a hearing will constitute an affirmative 
defense for the tenant in the event that a rent increase 
occurs and an action for unlawful detainer is filed for 
failure to pay the increased rent. 
City Councilmembers Nighswonger, Molinari and Hensle 
either own rental property or are involved in the sale of 
rental properties in Montebello. 
Your question is whether 
they can make or participate in the making of the decision 
on the rent mediation ordinance. 


Henry S. Barbosa 
December 8, 1982 
Page 2 
As you are aware, a public official cannot make or 
participate in the making of a governmental decision in 
which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial 
interest. 
(Government Code Section 87100.) * 
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning 
of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the 
decision will have a material financial effect on: 
(a) 
Any business entity in which the 
official has an investment worth more than $1,000; 
(b) 
Any real property in which the official 
has an interest worth more than $1,000; or 
(c) 
Any source of income of $250 or more 
received within the 12 months prior to the 
decision. 
It has already been determined, in your conversations 
with Mr. Leidigh of our office, that the Councilmembers 
have one or more of the types of interests described in 
Section 87103. 
The only remaining question is whether it 
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision on the rent 
mediation ordinance will have a material financial effect 
on any of these interests. 
Based on the facts you provided me with, it does not 
appear that the proposed ordinance, if adopted, would 
materially affect the Councilmembers' interests. 
"Materiality" is defined in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702 
(copy enclosed). 
Because the mediation is nonbinding, and 
the only legal consequence of the ordinance is to give a 
tenant a possible affirmative defense in an unlawful 
detainer action, it appears that the ordinance will have 
little or no financial impact on the Councilmembers' 
interests. 
Thus, the Councilmembers may make and 
participate in the making of this decision. 
My conclusion is based on the general facts that you 
provided me with. 
If a specific Councilmember feels that 
*Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. 


Henry S. Barbosa 
December 8, 1982 
Page 3 
it is reasonably foreseeable that, under the facts of his 
or her individual situation, the ordinance could materially 
affect his or her interests, disqualification is required. 
If you have any additional questions, please feel free 
to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosure 
Very truly yours,

HENRY S, BARBOSA 
RALPH M.OCHOA 
MALCOLM S, SEGAL 
HERMAN SILLAS 
ROSE SLOAN 
TIMOTHY H. B,YARYAN 
Ms. Janice McLean 
Staff Attorney 
OCHOA S BARBOSA 
ATTORNEYS AT LAW 
OVIATT BUILDING 
617 SOUTH OLIVE STREET, SUITE 1010 
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90014 
(213) 622-9170 
November 30, 1982 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
SACRAMENTO OFFICE 
PARK EXECUTIVE BUILDING 
925 L STREET, SUITE 905 
SACRAM ENTO, CA 95814 
(916)447-3383 
Re: 
Conflict of Interest 
Council Members 
In Connection With Considerat 
Rent 
Mediation Ordinance 
Dear Ms. McLean: 
This will confirm our telephone conversation of 
November 29, 1982, relative to my request for an 
inion 
with regard to the following facts: 
The Ci ty of Montebello is a general law ci ty of 
approximately 50,000 population, just outside the ci 
of 
Los Angeles in Los Angeles County. 
In recent months 
questions have ar isen regarding the appropr iateness of 
some form of rent control over the rental market within 
the City of Montebello. 
Mr. Liedigh of your office has 
previously indicated that any councilmember who ei ther 
has real estate holdings in rental property or who is in 
the business of selling rental properties would 
be 
disqualified from participation. 
In the case of our City 
that meant that three members

Ms. Janice McLean 
November 30, 1982 
Page 2

The rent 
iation 
inance 
the 
following mechanisms: 
(1) upon application by ei ther 
party, both a landlord and a tenant would be required to 
attend a rent mediation conference, the results of which 
would be non-binding upon the parties; that is, if the 
landlord was dissatisfied with the determination made by 
the mediator I 
the landlord could never the 
ss proceed 
with whatever action had been previously indicated would 
occur i 
and 
(2) in the event that a landlord refused to 
attend the mediation conference if requested by a tenant, 
such refusal and failure to attend the conference would 
provide the tenant wi th an affirmative 
fense in the 
event that the landlord proceeded with the purported rent 
increase and thereafter took an action for 
unlawful 
detainer for failure to pay the increased rent. 
The 
ordinance as proposed does not contemplate any active 
intervention 
by 
the 
City other 
than 
to 
appoint 
a 
mediation 
board 
whose 
members 
would 
be 
trained 
in 
mediation and sit individually as hearing officers in 
mediations. 
In summary, therefore, the only compulsion 
upon 
a 
landlord 
would 
be 
to 
attend 
the 
mediation 
conference after which the landlord could take any action 
he intended to take. 
Based 
upon 
the 
foregoi 
descr iption 
of 
the 
mediation ordinance, I have taken the position that the 
lack of compulsion built into the mediation ordinance 
would not give rise to a recognizable interest on the 
part of the three members who might want to participate 
notwithstanding their holdings and business interests in 
the rental property mar 
t. It would appear to me that a 
rent mediation ordinance is one step removed from the 
compUlsive character of a rent moratorium ordinance such 
as would not disqualify any of the three council members

sections.conclusion: Thus, the Councilmembers may make and participate in the making of this decision. My conclusion is based on the general facts that you provided me with. If a specific Councilmember feels that it is reasonably foreseeable that, under the facts of his or her individual situation, the ordinance could materially affect his or her interests, disqualification is required.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether three Montebello City Council members with financial interests in rental properties can participate in voting on a proposed rent mediation ordinance that creates a nonbinding mediation board for landlord-tenant disputes.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Councilmembers may participate in the decision because the proposed ordinance would not have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on their rental property interests, as the mediation is nonbinding and provides only a potential affirmative defense to tenants. However, any individual Councilmember who believes the ordinance could materially affect their specific circumstances must recuse themselves.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (November 30, 1982) and FPPC response letter (December 8, 1982). Analysis focuses on the FPPC response. OCR quality is good with minor formatting artifacts. References Government Code Section 87100 and 87103 regarding conflict of interest standards.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to inquiry regarding whether three Montebello City Councilmembers with rental property interests can participate in a vote on a proposed nonbinding rent mediation ordinance. The FPPC concludes they may participate because the ordinance would have little or no material financial impact on their interests.
